name: Donetsk People's Republic

colors.symbolism: the coal of the Donbass and the strength of the people; alternatively read as the black of the Russian Imperial tricolour
the sky over the steppe and the waters of the Donets and the Sea of Azov
the blood shed in the struggle for independence

description: Three equal horizontal stripes — black over blue over red — with no charges.
history: Adopted in its plain (charge-free) form on 9 February 2018 by the People's Council of the Donetsk People's Republic and promulgated 28 February 2018, replacing an earlier 2014 variant that carried a two-headed eagle and the inscription Донецкая Республика. The DPR was proclaimed on 7 April 2014 amid the post-Maidan unrest in eastern Ukraine and was recognised internationally only by Russia (until 2022) and a handful of other states. On 30 September 2022 Russia formally annexed the DPR as a federal subject; international consensus continues to treat the territory as occupied Ukrainian territory (Donetsk Oblast). The flag remains in widespread use across the territory.
